products.name: Weber Spirit II E-310
products.verdict: The best gas grill for most people — Weber quality, 3 burners, and the GS4 grilling system at a fair price.
products.description: The Weber Spirit II E-310 is the gas grill that grilling experts recommend more than any other. Its GS4 grilling system — Infinity Ignition, burners, Flavorizer bars, and grease management — is engineered for consistent, flavorful results every time. Three burners provide 529 sq in of cooking space (enough for 15 burgers), and the open-cart design with side tables keeps tools within reach. Weber's 10-year warranty and massive accessory ecosystem seal the deal.
pros: GS4 system delivers consistently great results
529 sq in handles family-size cookouts
Weber build quality is legendary
Huge accessory ecosystem
cons: No sear zone or infrared burner

products.name: Traeger Ironwood 650
products.verdict: The best pellet grill — WiFIRE smart control, D2 drivetrain, and Super Smoke mode for competition-level results.
products.description: The Traeger Ironwood 650 is the pellet grill that turns backyard cooks into pitmasters. Its D2 Direct Drive drivetrain maintains precise temperature within 5°F, and WiFIRE connectivity lets you monitor and adjust from your phone anywhere. Super Smoke mode maximizes wood-fired flavor at low temperatures, and the downdraft exhaust system ensures even heat across all 650 sq in of cooking space. If you want set-it-and-forget-it smoking with smart features, Traeger wrote the book.
pros: WiFIRE lets you control from anywhere
Super Smoke mode adds incredible flavor
D2 drivetrain maintains ±5°F precision
650 sq in handles large cooks
cons: Doesn't sear as well as gas or charcoal

products.name: Weber Master-Touch 22"
products.verdict: The best charcoal grill — the iconic Weber kettle perfected with a hinged grate and ash catcher.
products.description: The Weber Master-Touch is the charcoal grill that's been the backyard standard for decades, refined to near-perfection. The 22" kettle provides 363 sq in of cooking space with a hinged cooking grate that lets you add charcoal without removing food. The One-Touch cleaning system sweeps ash into a removable catcher below, and the built-in thermometer monitors dome temperature. It's simple, effective, and produces that unmistakable charcoal flavor no gas grill can replicate.
features: Hinged cooking grate for easy charcoal access
pros: Unmistakable charcoal flavor
Hinged grate is a game-changer for charcoal
One-Touch ash cleanup is easy
Incredibly durable — lasts decades
cons: Charcoal requires more effort than gas
No side burner or work surface
363 sq in is smaller than gas grills

products.name: Napoleon Prestige 500
products.verdict: The luxury gas grill — infrared rear burner, 900 sq in of space, and stainless steel construction throughout.
products.description: The Napoleon Prestige 500 is what happens when you apply premium engineering to a gas grill. Four main burners plus an infrared rear rotisserie burner and an infrared sear station deliver 80,000+ BTU across 900 sq in of total cooking space. Stainless steel construction, iconic WAVE reversible cast-iron grids, and the JETFIRE ignition system ensure restaurant-quality results. It's the grill for serious cooks who want the best and don't mind paying for it.
features: 4 main burners + infrared rear + sear station
pros: Infrared sear station produces steakhouse-quality sears
900 sq in handles the biggest parties
Stainless steel build is durable and beautiful
Reversible grids for different cooking styles
cons: Propane consumption is high at full power

products.name: Weber Go-Anywhere Charcoal Grill
products.verdict: The best portable grill — foldable legs, 160 sq in of cooking space, and classic Weber quality for under $60.
products.description: The Weber Go-Anywhere is the grill you throw in the car for camping, tailgating, or beach trips. Its rectangular design provides 160 sq in of cooking space — enough for 6 burgers — and the foldable legs lock the lid shut for easy transport. Porcelain-enameled steel resists rust and retains heat, and the adjustable dampers give you temperature control. At $59, it's the cheapest Weber and the best portable charcoal grill by a wide margin.
features: Foldable legs lock lid for transport
pros: $59 for genuine Weber quality
Foldable design is truly portable
160 sq in fits 6 burgers
Adjustable dampers for temp control
cons: 160 sq in is small for groups

products.name: Camp Chef Woodwind Wi-Fi 24
products.verdict: The best value pellet grill — Slide and Grill technology lets you smoke AND sear on one machine.
products.description: The Camp Chef Woodwind Wi-Fi 24 solves the biggest pellet grill weakness: searing. Its patented Slide and Grill technology opens a direct-flame zone so you can sear steaks over real fire — something most pellet grills can't do. Wi-Fi connectivity with the Camp Chef app lets you monitor cooks remotely, and PID temperature control maintains ±5°F accuracy. The Ash Kickin cleanout system makes post-cook cleanup a breeze. At $799, it undercuts Traeger while adding sear capability.
pros: Slide and Grill searing is a game-changer
Wi-Fi monitoring from your phone
PID control is precise and reliable
Ash cleanout is the easiest in the industry
cons: 570 sq in is smaller than Traeger Ironwood
Sear zone doesn't get as hot as dedicated gas
Build quality is good but not premium

products.name: Big Green Egg Large
products.verdict: The ultimate kamado grill — ceramic insulation holds heat for hours and produces the best smoked and grilled food possible.
products.description: The Big Green Egg is the kamado grill that started a cult following. Its thick ceramic shell retains heat incredibly efficiently, maintaining temperature for 12+ hours on a single load of charcoal. It grills, smokes, roasts, bakes, and even makes pizza — all with superior heat retention and moisture that produces the juiciest results of any grill type. The Large size handles a 20 lb turkey or 12 burgers. It's an investment, but owners universally say it's the last grill they'll ever buy.
features: 12+ hours on a single charcoal load
pros: Ceramic retains heat better than any material
12+ hours of cooking on one load of charcoal
Produces the juiciest, most flavorful food
Lasts a lifetime — ceramic is nearly indestructible
cons: 262 sq in is small for the price

products.name: Char-Broil Performance Series 4-Burner
products.verdict: The best budget gas grill — 4 burners, 425 sq in, and a side burner for under $300.
products.description: The Char-Broil Performance Series proves you can get a full-featured gas grill without breaking the bank. Four stainless steel burners plus a side burner deliver 36,000 BTU across 425 sq in of primary cooking space. The lidded side burner handles sauces and sides, porcelain-coated grates resist sticking, and the electronic ignition starts every time. At under $300 with a side burner and enclosed cart, it's the most grill you can get for the money.
pros: Under $300 for a 4-burner with side burner
Enclosed cart provides storage
Porcelain grates resist sticking
Electronic ignition is reliable
cons: Build quality is adequate, not premium
Rust can develop if not covered

products.name: Blackstone 36" Outdoor Griddle
products.verdict: The best outdoor griddle — 720 sq in of flat-top cooking surface for breakfast, smash burgers, and stir-fry.
products.description: The Blackstone 36" Griddle isn't a traditional grill — it's a flat-top griddle that opens up an entirely different world of outdoor cooking. Smash burgers, eggs, pancakes, stir-fry, fajitas, fried rice — anything you'd cook on a restaurant flat-top, you can cook here. Four independently controlled burners provide 60,000 BTU across a massive 720 sq in cooking surface. The cold-rolled steel top develops a natural non-stick seasoning over time, getting better with every cook.
features: 720 sq in cold-rolled steel cooking surface
pros: 720 sq in is the largest cooking surface available
Flat-top opens up cooking options grills can't do
Seasoning builds natural non-stick over time
4 heat zones for different temperatures
cons: Not a traditional grill — no grill marks
Heavy and large — needs dedicated space
No lid — can't do indirect/convection cooking

products.name: Weber Q 2200
products.verdict: The best portable gas grill — one-burner simplicity with Weber quality for patios, balconies, and tailgates.
products.description: The Weber Q 2200 is the premium portable gas grill for people who want propane convenience in a compact package. Its single burner delivers 12,000 BTU across 280 sq in of porcelain-enameled cast-iron cooking grates — enough for 8 burgers. The push-button ignition, built-in thermometer, and infinite burner control make it dead simple to use. Compact enough for apartment balconies yet capable enough for tailgates and camping trips, it's the go-anywhere gas grill.
features: Compatible with Weber Q stand (sold separately)
pros: Weber quality in a portable gas format
Cast-iron grates produce great grill marks
Simple one-burner operation
Perfect for balconies and small spaces
